notes: Roda muito bem com alguns pequenos problemas como: input Lag(delay entre tocar e a saida do audio, quase impersepitivel), algumas travadas raras (ainda não presenciei mas a relatos que acontece). 1: você vai presisar colocar o som como o ALSA, abra o terminal e digite:  WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa (se ele não reconhecer o codigo vc ele ira dizer para vc instalar o winetricks). 2: Depois precisara mudar o local do seu dico Z no winecfg vá até o terminal e digite: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winecfg Vá ate  a aba, unidades e, clique do disco Z e coloque o seguinte caminho: /home/(Nome de usuario)/.steam/. 3: Apartir daqui seu rocksmith teoricamente já deve executar mas sem audio pois falta os drivers de audio 32 bits, abra o terminal: sudo apt-get install libasound2:i386 libasound2-plugins:i386. 4: Vá até os arquivo Rocksmith.ini na pasta raiz do jogo (talvez precise iniciar o rocksmith pelo menos 1 vez para ele ser criado), e verifique se as linhas ExclusiveMode=0 e Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0 estão com o valor 0. 5: E por ultimo vc precisara baixar o pavucontrol ir na aba configurações e colocar o Rocksmth guitar adapter como desligado (sem isso o som da guitarra não sai no jogo), o jogo roda muito bem sem problemas de performance em alta qualidade (talvez presencie alguns erros graficos aq e ali mas nada que seja realmente incomode) você pode tocar as musicas e o mais importante as custom songs funcionam perfeitamente (erros de vc entrar em uma custom song e ela crashar o game é normal isso é problema da propria musica acontece o msm no windows),e tambem é possivel se conectar com a Uplay. Recomendo que leia os outros reports em caso de erros e tals e informações mais tecnicas já qu eu não sou um exímio usuario de linux, mais uma coisa o modo lições pode não funcionar direito pois ele t força a tocar a nota do jeito certo e dependendo de como a sua guitarra esta configurada vc pode acabar impacado com o rocksmith não sabendo se vc está ou não tocando a nota certa (devido a o aumento da distorção da guitarra no linux já que o real tone cable não esta devidamente configurado o linux configura o real tone cable como mic padrão, caso isso acontecer tente deixar a guitarra mais limpa e com som mais agudo, q isso pode ajudar o rocksmith a reconhecer a nota certa e assim vc não fica empacado e tendo que reiniciar o jogo)
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
Similar tweaks to the other Mint users here. When it was all up and running the guitar model was missing, but this doesn't affect gameplay much. 

(1) $ WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa

(2) $ WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winecfg

(3) In winecfg, went to the Drives tab and mapped Z: to ~/.steam

(4) Had some issues with wine not being able configure drives, but uninstalling and reinstalling Rocksmith in steam fixed that up.

(5) To detect the realtone cable, installed pavucontrol, on the Configuration tab set the Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to Off

(6) To get audio working, had to install libasound2:i386 libasound2-plugins:i386

(7) Thanks for the tips from all the other reporters!
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
I followed the instructions everybody gave here in the other reports and it worked great. I felt the same audio lag as I felt playing on Windows. This happens PROBABLY because I'm playing on an old monitor with early HDMI support, which means low bandwidth and high latency.
To fix this problem, I connected a p2 audio output into a generic sound system (guitar amplifier, PC speakers, sound bar) and my experience has improved DRAMATICALLY! I only need to set input latency on in-game options between 10 and 20 and everything is smooth as it should be!
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: The instructions everyone's following didn't quite work for me. A few changes were needed to get this to work for me. Set sound to alsa and bring up winecfg as usual, but I needed to set my Z drive to ~/.local/share/Steam or else steam was unable to locate the game exe. The standard changes to Rocksmith.ini were needed. Audio was horribly garbled until I made changes to /etc/pulse/daemon.conf: default-fragments = 5 and default-fragment-size-msec = 2. Additionally, I couldn't see the graphic to tune my guitar until disabled d3d11. After all is said and done, the game runs fine, now. There's minor input lag, but it's hardly a show-stopper.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
Initially attempting to launch after installing the game results in a "Something went wrong" window popping up, with a link to a webpage providing zero help (this appears to be part of the original game executable). What you need to do is this:
1. If you haven't already, run "sudo apt install wine-development winetricks". This will install Wine and Winetricks to your system, which will allow you to tweak Steam's settings for running the game.
2. Run "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa". This tells Proton (through Winetricks) to use the Alsa sound system, which appears to be essential for getting audio to work in this game.
3. Locate your game's Rocksmith2014.exe file. The path to this file should look like [SOMETHING]/steamapps/common/Rocksmith2014/Rocksmith2014.exe. Note what the [SOMETHING] is. For me, this was /home/user/.steam/steam
4. Run WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winecfg. This will open a window to configure Wine for this particular game. At the top, select the Drives tab. Click on the Z: and notice the box below that says Path. In this box, put in your [SOMETHING] from step 3. At this point, you should be able to launch the game.

From there, I'm stuck. No matter what I do, the game tells me to connect my Rocksmith Real Tone Cable. However, the cable is plugged in. Running lsusb confirms that my computer sees it. I'm also able to listen to the cable as an input in Audacity. Other users have suggested installing "pavucontrol", and then going to the Configuration tab and setting the Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to "Off", but this has not had any benefit to me. While the game seems to launch great and all the menus and stuff work, the failure to recognize the cable places this solidly in the Bronze category for me.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: 1) Set sound to alsa: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa   2) Symlink ~/.local/share/Steam/userdata to / (mapping Z drive did not work for me): sudo ln -s ~/.local/share/Steam/userdata /   3) Open Rocksmith.ini in Steamapps Rocksmith folder and set the following: ExclusiveMode=0 and Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0   4) In pavucontrol -> configuration: set Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to off   5) Open /etc/pulse/daemon.conf and set the following: default-fragments = 5 and default-fragment-size-msec = 2   6) Disable D3D11. See the "Here's how"
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
Just as other reporters said: Setting the prefix to ALSA sound, disabling the Real Tone cable, and disabling d3d11 are all necessary, along with the INI tweaks.  I couldn't get the game to launch unless I installed it in ~/.steam/steam/steamapps and set the Z: drive to /home/user/.steam/steam/ 
If you prefer your games on a seperate drive, you're kinda screwed. Even moving the game and then symlinking to it results in a crash on startup on my end. 
The minor sound lag is still an issue. It depends on how you play - personally I can adjust to the lag after a few minutes if I have noise cancelling headphones.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
1) Set sound to alsa: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa
2) WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winecfg 
 mapping Z: to ~/.steam/steam (match this to your steam library folder)
3) Open Rocksmith.ini in Steamapps Rocksmith folder and set the following: ExclusiveMode=0 and Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0
4) In pavucontrol -> configuration: set Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to off
5) Open /etc/pulse/daemon.conf and set the following: default-fragments = 5 and default-fragment-size-msec = 2

There remains a little lag for the guitar output, but I can get used to it.

Thank to all previous effort.

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1070 with Max-Q Design
notes: The game launches. No need for disabling D3d11 or mounting home folder (this used to be useful long time ago). The game isn't playable, though. The problem is the incompatibility with win32-low-latency feature (you can test the behaviour by disabling this option on a windows installation of the game and hear the resemblance for yourself). The lag isn't that bad (even though after a some time it starts growing and becomes noticeable) but the guitar tones are incredibly distorted. Tried every trick in the book from optimizing alsa input latency to nicing the process, using different kernels (low-latency too), so forth. The results vary depending on these settings but not that much and not when it comes to sound quality anyway. I really hope the devs are looking into this problem (win32-low-latency compatibility) because i'm afraid we won't ever be able to fully play this title without it. Also, an automation of default using alsa instead of pulse (unless they have something in mind regarding the later... who knows?!) would be nice. Until then, let's be honest and give this title the rating it deserves. So Bronze it is for me since the game is not really playable without a masive headache.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: This is an update to an earlier report where I claimed gold status. This game has grown increasingly unstable with time. First I'd sometimes need to start the game twice due to strange graphical glitches. Then it became a '2-3 times is the charm' affair. Then 4-5. Still, once you got it going, it was normally okay. Now the game sometimes crashes aftera few songs even if everything seems to be perfect. This title appears to be regressing.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes: Following the tweaks listed here by other users got this working for me. (Set audio to ALSA, remap Z: drive, edit Rocksmith.ini) Oddly enough, I didn't need as much tweaking as others did. Perhaps this is because I'm using microphone mode with an acoustic guitar, rather than the Real Tone cable. Also worth noting is that microphone mode is more accurate here than on my Windows laptop, although that may be because I can position it better at my desktop. A few notes: Even the Windows version refuses to acknowledge webcam microphones, and the kind of microphone that comes built-in on most laptops probably won't be enough. Also, I am most certainly not playing on the highest difficulties, so take what I say with a grain of salt if you've played guitar before.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
I got it working, even with a different audio interface instead of the realtone cable. However *latency* makes this not really playable. Hence I give it a silver.

What I did to get it working, mostly the same as other findings:
1. Winetricks to set alsa for audio.
2. Install the game to your ~/.steam library and map it to the Z: drive. (Tried another library location, but "Sorry, something went wrong" dialogs kept coming no matter how I mapped the Z: drive.)
3. Tweak Rocksmith.ini to get audio working and minimize latency.

These were my findings when tweaking:
DumpAudioLog=1, I found this very helpful tweaking. Find the log in audiodump.txt in the same folder as the ini.
Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0, or it will fail with lines looking like this in the audiodump.txt
SoundInputPortAudio::InputOn Failed to open stream, err -9996
This is the main reason I don't have high hopes for playable latency, as this is the essential setting on windows for achieving that (played ~180 hours on windows).
ForceWDM=0, setting to 1 fails to detect any devices.
ExclusiveMode=0, setting it to 1 does work for me but has higher latency (2048 sample buffer needed vs 1440 without exclusive mode).
LatencyBuffer=2, this is the lowest it will go for me before crackling. This depends on your CPU performance so tweak to your situation.
ForceDirectXSink=0/1, has no effect for me at all. audiodump also doesn't pick up this setting and displays 0 when in the ini it's set to 1.
RealToneCableOnly=0/1, no latency effect.
EnableMicrophone=0/1, no latency effect.

Using an alternative audio interface worked for me with phobos2077's no cable hack v2.
1. Rename Rocksmith2014.exe to something else, I used CoreRocksmith2014.exe
2. Place the nocable exe and rename it to Rocksmith2014.exe
3. Place the nocable ini file and rename it to Rocksmith2014.ini
4. Update the ini file to your needs.
5. Start game through steam as usual.

The changes you need to make to the ini file:
[general]
processName=CoreRocksmith2014.exe
; Find and set the vendor ID and product ID of your usb interface.
[offsets]
; Make sure you have all the latest offsets. I have a total of 5. (offsetVID_0 to offsetVID_4)

title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes:
Recently, I wrote that I can't hear any sound when I set it to ALSA. After reinstalling my OS, it works (might have messed around with the sound drivers before, idk).

Set sound to alsa, map the Z: drive, edit the Rocksmith.ini, disable D3D11, set the RealTone Cable to off in pavucontrol (other users explained it in detail). You can make the changes to /etc/pulse/daemon.conf, but it didn't make a difference for me. I also can't find a difference between using Proton 3.16-9 and 4.11-6.

It runs, but the sound is distorted. The portuguese report confirmed that this is common. Also, the delay from plucking a string until you can hear something is about 10ms or even higher, which is unacceptable for me.

Learn a song works (This is the main criteria, as I don't really use any other mode) and CDLCs work too. If it wasn't for the delay, I would be happy.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

notes:
1) Set sound to alsa: 
'WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa'
2) Map the library to your root location:
'sudo ln -s ~/.local/share/Steam/userdata /'
3) Open Rocksmith.ini in Steamapps Rocksmith folder and set the following: 
ExclusiveMode=0 and Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0 
4) In pavucontrol -> configuration: 
set Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to off 
5) Open /etc/pulse/daemon.conf and set the following: 
default-fragments = 5 and default-fragment-size-msec = 2 
6) Disable D3D11

RESULTS: Everything works, but the input lag is slightly bigger and the gain is a little bit smaller.
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered

cpu: AMD A10-7870K Radeon R7, 12 Compute Cores 4C+8G
notes:
1) WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/221680/pfx winetricks sound=alsa          

2) sudo ln -s ~/.local/share/Steam/userdata /          

3) To ge the USB working 
nano ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Rocksmith2014/Rocksmith.ini          

ExclusiveMode=0
Win32UltraLowLatencyMode=0 

4) In pavucontrol -> configuration: set Rocksmith Guitar Adapter to off           

5) sudo nano /etc/pulse/daemon.conf 
set the following: default-fragments = 5 and default-fragment-size-msec = 2           

6) Change the runtime configuration. Use the Set Launch Options setting in the game's Properties dialog in the Steam client

PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%
title: Rocksmith® 2014 Edition - Remastered
